description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, angular body and a convex glass screen. The television stands on four slender, angled legs, giving it a mid-century modern appearance. Two metal antenna rods protrude from the top, and a power cord extends from the back, ending in a plug. The front features a large, rounded rectangular screen with a slightly greenish tint, flanked by two round control knobs below the screen. The casing is primarily dark, with a textured, matte finish, and the rear panel is faceted and brownish, suggesting a plastic or Bakelite material. The overall design is utilitarian yet iconic, reminiscent of televisions from the 1950s or 1960s.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a convex glass screen, metal antennas, and angled legs, featuring a dark matte casing and retro control knobs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex screen, two control knobs, and metal antennas on top.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the television and the protruding back panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the faceted brown back panel, antennas, and the power cord extending outward.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the screen's curvature and the power cord trailing from the back.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, black, matte plastic bezel with rounded corners and no visible branding
attribute_control_button_layout: two round control knobs located on the lower front panel, arranged in a horizontal row
